title: Pods, Multi-Container Composition, Lifecycle, Termination, and Debug Containers · Cluster Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in pods, multi-container composition, lifecycle, termination, and debug containers, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Explain Pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement.
Choose init, app, native sidecar, adapter, ambassador, and helper containers from startup, restart, ownership, shutdown, and resource contracts.
Trace waiting, running, terminated, reasons, exit codes, restartPolicy, backoff, previous state, and Pod phase without equating them.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Pod Shared Boundaries
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Pod has an init container that never completes, a sidecar that outlives the app, an ephemeral debug container, restart count 2, and an emptyDir holding the only diagnosis. Explain phase, container state, restart, sharing, and replacement boundaries. Competency probe: Explain Pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement.

Inspect the K8s Pod Shared Boundaries trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

For Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Pod Shared Boundaries, keep K8s Pod Shared Boundaries connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-ephemeral-debug-container-limits-1hr9ad". Tailor its pod.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits, keep K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-ephemeral-debug-container-limits-1hr9ad
  - competency: k8s-ephemeral-debug-container-limits
    artifact: pod.yaml
    decision: ephemeral debug container limits
    environment: remote context with short-lived federated workload identity
    fault-injection: rotate a projected token while the API dependency slows
    retained-proof: Pod state previous exit endpoint readiness and request result
    case-key: 1hr9ad
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Pod
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      initContainers: [schema-check]
      containers: [api, telemetry-sidecar]
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous -c api
    failure: Reject durable-Pod assumptions, unbounded sidecars, swallowed signals, missing previous logs, or debug mutation presented as image repair.
    repair: Assign container ownership, bound startup and shutdown, capture states and exit causes, and prove a clean replacement preserves required behavior.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use ephemeral containers and shared process evidence for bounded debugging while preserving image immutability and naming remaining node-level limits.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug Pod has an init container that never completes, a sidecar that outlives the app, an ephemeral debug container, restart count 5, and an emptyDir holding the only diagnosis. Explain phase, container state, restart, sharing, and replacement boundaries. Competency probe: Use ephemeral containers and shared process evidence for bounded debugging while preserving image immutability and naming remaining node-level limits.

For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: An ephemeral debug container can modify the original image and proves the clean replacement will work.
